title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-data automation team integrating a staging catalog must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for timeout layers, deadlines, cancellation, cleanup, and budget allocation, producing end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers end-to-end request budget and cancellation model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Timeout Layers
steps.instruction:
workshop case — civic-data staging importer has 450 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Timeout Layers.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate name resolution, connect, tls, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. demonstrate the result through python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports. For Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Timeout Layers, keep Timeout Layers connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
